rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BERNARD M FOSTON SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Pay Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Former Attorney For Conservator; Memo P/A; Declaration Of Mark S Drobny
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/29/24 to be heard with the new accounting petition. Issue(s) that must be addressed before 4/29/24: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) This Court does not favor internal conferences. Some of Mr. Lachona's time entries appear to be a little high, such as 3.2 hours on 5/12/21 to edit petitions drafted by the paralegal, .6 hours on 5/26/21 for an internal conference with the paralegal regarding filing the petitions, and 1.2 hours on 6/8/21 for an internal conference with the paralegal and attending to notice issues, among a few others. (3) The petition does not set forth who the interested parties and their addresses are for the Court to determine if notice was properly served. Since the conservatee is now deceased, who is his successor-in-interest? Has a personal representative been appointed for his estate? Did he have a trust? When the successor conservator's accounting petition is heard, an appearance might be required for the Court to determine if fees requested are reasonable. Please provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 4/19/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAPHNE CARUANA GALIZIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Final Distribution And Closing Of Insolvent Estate, Approving Waiver Of Administrator'S Statutory Fees; Approving Waiver Of Statutory Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/29/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 03/19/2024). The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) No inventory and appraisal (showing $0 property) is on file. It must be filed even if there is no property subject to administration, to comply with Probate Code section 8800(d). 3) Settlement and approval of the first an final accounting must be included in the petition caption and item 1 of the DE-120 form as additional relief sought. CRC 7.50 & 102. 4) The beginning date of the first account must be the date of death. LRSF 14.34. 5) The first account period cannot exceed 2 years from the date of death. No court approval has been sought to enlarge the account period. LRSF 14.34(A). Note: An appearance may be required on 04/29/2024, unless papers are filed to show satisfactory progress to address the outstanding issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GUILLERMO COBAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/22/2024 (no earlier date available). Re the DE-120 form filed on 03/18/2024: i) The address of the attorney for Bridge Financial Funding listed on the proof of service is still incorrect (1350 Treat Blvd., Suite 410, Walnut Creek, CA 94597). ii) Item 1 referred to the "revised" petition, but the petition has not been amended. The following issues (see tentative ruling for 03/04/2024 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) The date of giving notice to the Franchise Tax Board must be specified. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 2) Please provide a copy of notice of proposed action given before donating the estate's tangible persona property. See Probate Code section 10513. The information required by CRC 7.250(a) must also be provided. 3) The schedule of disbursements does not show payment of the probate referee's commission or court filing fees, and no reimbursement of such costs has been sought. Who has advanced those undisclosed expenses of administration? 4) No bank statement has been filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). 5) The estate cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. Please provide a proof that the cash is now fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 10533(a)(2). 6) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee and assignee. The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 7) The first account period cannot exceed 2 years from the date of death. No prior court approval has been obtained to enlarge the account period. LRSF 14.34(A). 8) Is the estate ready to be closed, even though the administrator has not recovered proceeds of Macy shares from the Controller?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. An interested party in Probate proceedings is entitled to direct notice. If she is represented by an attorney, the attorney also receives notice but, absent an express direction that notice be given only to the attorney, both must receive notice. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b).] 2. The interested party must consent in writing to receive electronic service, not her attorney. Did Mary Bong expressly consent to electronic service? [CRC 2.251.] 3. The supplement did not address the objections e-filed on 12/4/23. Deadline is 4/29/24 to deliver a hard copy of the proposed order and courtesy copies of any new filings to the assigned examiner. (Please do not prepare another supplement that reads like a "test answer sheet;" issues should just be addressed by providing the correct notice and filing proof of service, or by dropping off the proposed order, or by filing the consent, etc.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (No courtesy copy of the supplement filed on 3/13/24 was received by the examiner, despite what was alleged in that supplement).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADA DEGHETTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The decedent died in 2002, more than 22 years ago, but no interim accounts have been filed. The first account must start with the date of death in 2002, but the first account period cannot exceed 2 years. The second and each subsequent account period cannot exceed 3 years. LRSF 14.34(A). Absent waivers of account signed by all beneficiaries, the executor will be required to file 7 interim accounting petitions before final distribution. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROY AND JUDY QUICHO FAMILY TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 23, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate Code Section 850 Petition For Order Confirming Trust Ownership Of Personal Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The settlors left two joint bank accounts titled in their individual names: Wells Fargo savings account 0604; and checking account 9275. The savings account 0604 was specifically listed on "Schedule A" attached to the trust. The checking account 9275, however, did not exist when the settlors signed their trust and assignment of personal property to the trust in 2002, because the account was not opened until 2010. The assignment to the trust signed in 2002 specifically mentioned "checking accounts", whether "now owned or hereafter acquired", as part of personal property intended to be transferred "without consideration" to the trust. The settlors used to have another Wells Fargo checking account 1643. The checking account 1643 was specifically listed on Schedule A attached to the trust because the account was in existence when the trust was created. The account was closed shortly after the checking account 9275 was opened in 2010. The 2010 account statements show the settlors only transferred $100 + $4.18 from the checking account 1643 to the checking account 9275. Restatement (Third) of Trusts section 41 provides in relevant part that "an interest that has not come into existence cannot be held in trust. A person can make a promise to create a trust of an interest in such a thing should it thereafter be acquired, but such an agreement is not binding unless the requirements of the law of contracts are satisfied. If consideration is received for a purported declaration of trust or assignment in trust of a bare expectancy or nonexistent property, the purported declaration or transfer is treated as a contract to create a trust even though it is worded as a present declaration or transfer. No trust arises when the person later acquires the intended trust property in the absence of some express or implied manifestation at the later time of an intent to give effect to the trust." See also Karras v. Karras (Ohio.Ct.App.2016) 76 N.E. 3d 706, 713-714 ["While acknowledging the general rule that later-acquired property is not trust property, they assert that Andreas' and Ourania's declaration of trust with respect to later-acquired property is enforceable, as a matter of contract law, because it was supported by consideration --- As set forth above, the 1992 assignment that transferred property into the accompanying trust explicitly stated that the assignment of assets was "without consideration."] See also Kucker v. Kucker (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 90, 95 ["A form provided by the practice guide for use when the client has assumed full responsibility for funding the trust can be modified to advise the client to return periodically to execute a general assignment of all or substantially all of their assets to the trust so that a Heggstad petition can be used to capture any later acquired items not titled in the name of the trust."] PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
